title: What Restaurants will be like in 20 - 25 Years Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a video request about the future of restaurants, predicting they will become more culturally central, less franchised, more individualistic, and chef-driven as society becomes wealthier. He also shares opinions on tipping culture and the social/cultural role restaurants have always played.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: restaurants are a holdout industry that won't be taken over by automation due to their intrinsically human and cultural nature
restaurants will become more important, less franchised, more individualistic, and chefs will become celebrities
tipping should be abolished — it creates awkward social pressure and workers should just be paid what the job is worth
future restaurants will blend cuisines more extensively, creating new ethnic food combinations
restaurants are and will remain a central pillar of culture and social gathering
Kyle himself eats canned food to save money and doesn't go to restaurants
